summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special zoning workshop with the Zoning Commission, Board of Zoning Appeals, and Clermont County Planner Drew Demarche. Demarche presented on a proposed land use plan for the township, covering county development trends, community surveys, and corridor planning goals. The board discussed potential zoning resolution amendments including minimum lot sizes for planned developments (PDR/PDM), setback requirements, overlay districts vs. base zoning for PUDs, mixed-use density ratios, and garage/yard sale regulations. The board approved township staff working with county staff to create a land use plan.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve Township staff to work with Clermont County staff to create a land use plan – passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 7:42 p.m. – passed unanimously
action_items: Township staff (Director Langenheim) to work with Clermont County staff (Drew Demarche) to develop a land use plan
Drew Demarche to research community survey methods including EDDM (Every Door Direct Mail) for community input
Director Langenheim to research overlay district approach for PUDs and formulate potential language changes
Director Langenheim to research the 60/40 residential-to-commercial split for PDM districts to ensure it doesn't limit the township
Staff to draft garage/yard sale regulations including a three-consecutive-day limit and complaint-based enforcement
Staff to look into transient vendor regulations for roadside sellers
key_amounts: Current PDR minimum lot size: 5,000 square feet
Current most dense lot size (R5): 12,000 square feet
Miami Township minimum lot size: 8,400 square feet with 70-foot minimum lot width
Half-acre lot size reference: 21,780 square feet
Current PUD side setback: 5 feet from property line (10 feet between facades)
Proposed setback increase: 15 feet from property line (30 feet between facades)
Proposed PDM split: 60% max residential, 40% commercial
Proposed lot size increase to 5,800 square feet for PDR
notable_topics: Land use plan development with Clermont County
County housing study and economic development plan
PDR/PDM minimum lot sizes and setback requirements
Overlay districts vs. base zoning layer for PUDs
State Route 28 corridor planning and traffic concerns with ODOT
Garage and yard sale regulation to prevent junk accumulation
Transient vendor regulations for roadside sellers
Preserving rural character and farmland while managing growth
Building facade and material quality standards (brick construction)
notes: The transcript suffered from severe audio/transcription issues with repeated placeholder text ('Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.') appearing throughout, making significant portions of the discussion unrecoverable. Drew Demarche noted he previously directed Clinton County's Regional Planning Commission and started with Clermont County in February 2025. Williamsburg Township is also pursuing similar land use planning. The meeting was restricted to township employees and appointees only for discussion purposes.
title: BOT Zoning Workshop 9-8-25
